mission.title: Install and Configure Dify
mission.description: Dify is a production-ready platform for developing agentic workflows, designed for self-hosting on Kubernetes. It provides a robust framework for building AI applications with a focus on ease of use and scalability. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and resource management for optimal performance.

steps.title: Add the Dify Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Dify Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Dify using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add langgenius https://charts.langgenius.com

helm repo update

```

> This is the official Helm repository for Dify.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Dify CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required by Dify to manage its resources in Kubernetes.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs necessary for Dify.

steps.title: Install Dify using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Dify application in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install dify langgenius/dify --namespace dify --create-namespace --version 0.12.0

```

> Replace '0.12.0' with the desired version.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for Dify to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: Dify
metadata:
  name: example-dify
spec:
  workflow: example-workflow
EOF

```

> This command creates a sample Dify resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: Dify
metadata:
  name: example-dify
spec:
  workflow: example-workflow
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dify installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Dify pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n dify

```

> You should see the Dify pods in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dify
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Dify application and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with Dify.

```bash

helm uninstall dify --namespace dify

kubectl delete namespace dify

```

> This command uninstalls the Dify release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dify
upgrade.description:
This step outlines the process to upgrade Dify to a newer version, including a backup and verification step.

```bash

kubectl get all -n dify -o yaml > dify-backup.yaml

helm upgrade dify langgenius/dify --namespace dify --version 0.12.1

kubectl get pods -n dify

```

> Backup current Dify resources.
> Replace '0.12.1' with the desired version.
> Verify that the Dify pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n dify -o yaml > dify-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Dify not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dify Custom Resource may not have been created correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the Custom Resource definition using the provided YAML.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dify application may be misconfigured or missing required resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n dify' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm repository may not be added or updated correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo add langgenius https://charts.langgenius.com' and 'helm repo update'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Scale up your Kubernetes nodes or adjust resource requests in the Dify configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new workflow management features and improved API performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update existing workflows to the new format as per the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dify pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n dify'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: Dify
metadata:
  name: example-dify
spec:
  workflow: example-workflow
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
